source.title: Infralimbic cortex activity is required for the expression but not the acquisition of conditioned safety

evidence_direction_correction_note: Corrected 2026-05-04 from 'weakens' to 'supports'. Original direction was assigned during a MECH-302 lit pull where IL/vHipp-PL papers weaken the single-pipeline reading of MECH-302. But MECH-303 IS the parallel-substrate claim -- IL being the required substrate for safety-cue expression directly supports MECH-303.
confidence_rationale: IL is required for safety-cue expression (not acquisition), with PL controlled out. This establishes IL as a distinct substrate for predictive safety-cue encoding, parallel to the ventral-striatal relief-completion pathway -- exactly what MECH-303 claims. The acquisition-vs-expression dissociation maps to MECH-303's commitment-release gating function: the store can form without IL, but IL is required to read it out and release the avoidance commitment.
failure_signatures: If REE collapses 'relief-completion event' and 'this cue predicts relief' into a single mechanism, it will lose the distinction between just-now-happened and predicted-from-cue, which the IL/IL-vs-PL evidence here suggests is real.

The paper does not record from the IL during relief itself (only during conditioned safety expression), so the reverse claim — IL is *not* part of the completion-event circuit — is not strictly tested.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Temporary inactivation of the infralimbic cortex (IL) prior to safety conditioning does not impair acquisition. Inactivation of the IL during safety expression completely blocks startle-attenuation responses to the safety cue. Inactivation of the neighbouring prelimbic cortex (PL) during expression has no effect.
mapping.ree_translation: Encoding 'this cue predicts the absence/cessation of an aversive event' uses an IL-specific substrate that is dissociable from the ventral-striatal relief-completion pathway and from the adjacent PL. For REE this means a clean Model 1 reading would be inadequate: the completion event itself can reuse goal-achievement machinery, but the *predictive encoding* of safety cues (the analog of 'things liked' but for 'things that predict relief') likely needs its own substrate-equivalent rather than collapsing into the same store.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Conditioned safety here is operationalised as startle attenuation to a CS+ that follows non-occurrence of shock — closer to threat-absence than to relief at threat-termination per se. The Andreatta 2017 entry (separate from this slate) flags that threat-absence and threat-termination dissociate behaviourally. So the IL result here is most directly relevant to the threat-absence (safety-cue-prediction) version of the architectural question.
mapping.source_context: Adult male and female Sprague-Dawley rats; explicitly-unpaired CS-/US safety conditioning; IL or PL muscimol inactivation; startle response readout.

confidence_components.notes: Well-controlled (PL comparison, both sexes, acquisition-vs-expression dissociation). Mapping fidelity moderate because conditioned safety is one specific operationalisation of safety-signal encoding.
